documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/your-repo/ghost.git; docker run -it --rm -v $(pwd):/app ubuntu bash -c "cd /app && ./ghost.sh --dry-run". Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `chmod +x ghost.sh`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo ./ghost.sh --mode=stealth --users=all --dry-run --verbose`; `sudo ./ghost.sh --mode=super-stealth --patterns="Failed login,sudo" --services=apache,nginx --report=report.json`. GHOST v4.1 - Comprehensive Anti-Forensics Toolkit for Ethical Pentesting.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: GHOST v4.1 - Comprehensive Anti-Forensics Toolkit for Ethical Pentesting.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: GHOST v4.1 - Comprehensive Anti-Forensics Toolkit for Ethical Pentesting.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use ghost because GHOST v4.1 - Comprehensive Anti-Forensics Toolkit for Ethical Pentesting.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chmod +x ghost.sh` as documented upstream for ghost.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo ./ghost.sh --mode=stealth --users=all --dry-run --verbose` as documented upstream for ghost.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo ./ghost.sh --mode=super-stealth --patterns="Failed login,sudo" --services=apache,nginx --report=report.json` as documented upstream for ghost.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo ./ghost.sh --mode=restore --restore-from=/backup/path` as documented upstream for ghost.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair ghost with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ghost --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
